task_completion.description: Verify the required layers are created in LibreCAD.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel (usually on the right side of the LibreCAD interface) shows layers named FRAME, PICKHOLE, CENTERLINE, HATCH, DIMENSIONS, and TEXT.

task_completion.description: Verify the outer frame and cover circles are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two large concentric circles are visible in the main drawing area, representing the outer frame and the inner cover.

task_completion.description: Verify the pick holes and center lines are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two small circles (pick holes) are located vertically aligned inside the inner circle (one above the center, one below). A crosshair of horizontal and vertical center lines extends beyond the outer circle.

task_completion.description: Verify the ANSI31 hatch pattern is applied to the ring area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Diagonal parallel lines (ANSI31 hatch pattern) fill the annular space (the ring) between the outer frame circle and the inner cover circle.

task_completion.description: Verify diameter dimensions and required text are present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Diameter dimensions (showing 30, 24, and 1.5) are visible with dimension lines/arrows. The text 'MANHOLE FRAME & COVER' is placed above the drawing, and 'SCALE: NTS' is placed below it.

task_completion.description: Verify the drawing is saved to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar displays 'manhole_detail.dxf', or a save dialog/terminal confirms the file was saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/manhole_detail.dxf.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LibreCAD graphical interface to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreCAD application open with drawing tools, layers, and canvas being actively used.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a Python script (e.g., ezdxf) in the terminal to generate the DXF file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing process should be visible in the LibreCAD GUI, rather than terminal windows showing Python scripts being executed to bypass manual drawing.

integrity.description: Verify that dimensions and hatches were created using proper CAD tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Dimensions should have proper extension lines and arrows typical of CAD dimension tools, and the hatch should be uniformly bounded by the ring area, not manually drawn individual lines.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a LibreCAD drawing with specific layers: FRAME, PICKHOLE, CENTERLINE, HATCH, DIMENSIONS, and TEXT. The drawing must feature two concentric circles (radii 15 and 12) and two small pick holes (radius 0.75) vertically aligned at (0,6) and (0,-6). Center lines must extend from -18 to 18 horizontally and vertically. The ring between the two large circles must have an ANSI31 hatch pattern (diagonal lines). Dimensions for diameters 30, 24, and 1.5 must be present. Text 'MANHOLE FRAME & COVER' must be above, and 'SCALE: NTS' below. The final file must be saved as manhole_detail.dxf.
